experiment: Longitudinal latent change models compared mean-level personality changes across individuals who remained employed versus those experiencing unemployment (1-4 years duration) or reemployment, measured over 4 years with controls for confounding life events.

what_manipulated: number and congruency of observed hand movements
what_measured: reaction time and error rate on finger movement response
experiment: Within-subjects task where participants responded to cued finger movements while observing one or two hands performing movements congruent, incongruent, or neutral to the required response; congruency effects were analyzed to test whether motor system simultaneously integrates actions of multiple observed agents.

Meta-analytic comment examining selection bias and reporting biases in money-priming effect literature using funnel plots, selection models, excess significance tests, and p-curve analysis; non-empirical study.

what_measured: error-related negativity (ERN) and N2 event-related potential components
experiment: Cross-sectional Go/No-Go task with prepotent go response where EEG error-monitoring components (ERN, N2) were recorded during inhibition trials and correlated with political orientation measured via single self-report item.

what_manipulated: age group, video emotionality, question type
what_measured: theory of mind accuracy, metacognitive confidence, empathy rating, compassion rating
experiment: Mixed design comparing younger and older adults on a naturalistic video-based task (EmpaToM) with within-subject variation in emotionality (neutral vs. negative) and question type (mentalizing vs. factual reasoning); four social understanding facets measured simultaneously within each trial.

Cross-sectional online task where observers of varying ages viewed the young/old lady bistable image briefly (500 ms) and estimated its age; correlation between observer age and perceived age tested to assess whether own-age social in-group bias affects preconscious face perception.
